subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Diabetes and Slow Healing
description: A 60-second lesson on Diabetes and Slow Healing.

hook.text: A diabetic patient's foot wound won't heal for months. High blood sugar sabotages repair.

buildup.text: Diabetes damages small blood vessels, reducing blood flow to extremities.

discovery.text: High glucose levels impair white blood cell function and slow collagen production.

twist.text: Diabetic foot ulcers lead to over 70,000 amputations per year in the US alone.

climax.text: Careful wound care and blood sugar management can prevent most of these amputations.

punchline.text: Sugar doesn't just damage your diet. It damages your healing.

quiz.question: How does diabetes impair wound healing?
options: It damages blood vessels and impairs immune cell function
